reasoning: The question references a fictional scenario set in late 2025. According to simulated news reports, the October 26 peace accord was suspended by Thailand around November 12 following landmine injuries and border skirmishes that left 1 Cambodian villager dead. However, extensive searches reveal no additional fatalities between November 13 and November 23. The crowd forecast on November 13 was 9%, suggesting that forecasters aware of the initial November 12 incident still viewed a full relapse causing 20+ deaths as highly unlikely. Given the 10-day lull in casualties since the treaty suspension, the scenario currently lacks momentum toward the 20-death threshold. Still, the explicit suspension of the peace accord leaves the door open for a future scenario inject, keeping the probability slightly above the initial crowd baseline.
